description: Concurrent use significantly increases bleeding risk. Aspirin inhibits platelet aggregation and can displace warfarin from plasma proteins.
recommendation: Avoid combination. If necessary, monitor INR closely and watch for signs of bleeding.

description: NSAIDs inhibit platelet function and can cause GI bleeding, significantly enhancing warfarin's anticoagulant effect.
recommendation: Avoid concurrent use. Use acetaminophen for pain relief instead.

description: NSAIDs inhibit platelet function and increase GI bleeding risk, potentiating warfarin's anticoagulant effect.
recommendation: Avoid concurrent use. Monitor INR frequently if co-administration is unavoidable.

description: Metronidazole inhibits CYP2C9, reducing warfarin metabolism and significantly increasing anticoagulant effect.
recommendation: Reduce warfarin dose and monitor INR closely during and after metronidazole course.

description: Fluconazole is a potent CYP2C9 inhibitor that markedly elevates warfarin levels.
recommendation: Reduce warfarin dose by 25–50%. Monitor INR daily during fluconazole therapy.

description: Amiodarone and its metabolite inhibit warfarin metabolism via CYP2C9 and CYP3A4, increasing bleeding risk.
recommendation: Reduce warfarin dose by 30–50%. Monitor INR at least weekly for several months.

description: Amiodarone inhibits CYP3A4 and CYP2C9, raising simvastatin plasma levels and risk of myopathy/rhabdomyolysis.
recommendation: Limit simvastatin dose to 20 mg/day when co-administered with amiodarone.

description: Clarithromycin strongly inhibits CYP3A4, leading to greatly increased simvastatin exposure and myopathy risk.
recommendation: Suspend simvastatin during clarithromycin course. Resume after antibiotic is completed.

description: CYP3A4 inhibition by clarithromycin raises atorvastatin levels, increasing myopathy risk.
recommendation: Use lowest effective atorvastatin dose or temporarily switch to a non-CYP3A4-metabolised statin.

description: Alcohol potentiates metformin-associated lactic acidosis risk and can cause unpredictable hypoglycaemia.
recommendation: Limit alcohol intake. Avoid binge drinking entirely.

description: Iodinated contrast media can cause acute kidney injury, which prevents metformin clearance and raises lactic acidosis risk.
recommendation: Hold metformin 48 hours before and after iodinated contrast procedures. Confirm renal function before resuming.

description: Aspirin may blunt the blood-pressure-lowering effect of ACE inhibitors through prostaglandin inhibition.
recommendation: Use the lowest effective aspirin dose. Monitor blood pressure regularly.

description: ACE inhibitors reduce potassium excretion; supplemental potassium or potassium-sparing diuretics can cause hyperkalaemia.
recommendation: Monitor serum potassium levels regularly. Adjust supplementation accordingly.

description: Combining an ACE inhibitor with a potassium-sparing diuretic markedly increases hyperkalaemia risk.
recommendation: Monitor potassium and renal function closely, especially in elderly or diabetic patients.

description: NSAIDs reduce renal prostaglandin synthesis, decreasing the antihypertensive and renoprotective effects of ACE inhibitors.
recommendation: Avoid regular NSAID use. Use acetaminophen for analgesia. Monitor blood pressure and renal function.

description: Amlodipine inhibits CYP3A4, increasing simvastatin exposure and myopathy risk.
recommendation: Limit simvastatin to 20 mg/day when co-administered with amlodipine.

description: Combined negative chronotropic and dromotropic effects can cause severe bradycardia, heart block, or cardiac arrest.
recommendation: Avoid combination. If necessary, monitor ECG and heart rate continuously.

description: Additive depression of SA and AV node conduction may result in bradycardia and hypotension.
recommendation: Monitor heart rate and blood pressure. Use lower doses of each agent.

description: Fluoxetine inhibits CYP2D6, the primary enzyme metabolising metoprolol, causing 4–6× increase in metoprolol levels.
recommendation: Monitor for bradycardia, fatigue, and hypotension. Consider reducing metoprolol dose.

description: Omeprazole inhibits CYP2C19, the enzyme that converts clopidogrel to its active metabolite, reducing antiplatelet efficacy.
recommendation: Use pantoprazole (weaker CYP2C19 inhibitor) instead of omeprazole when a PPI is required.

description: Dual antiplatelet therapy increases bleeding risk, although the combination is intentional in certain cardiovascular indications.
recommendation: Only use together when prescribed for a specific cardiovascular indication. Monitor for bleeding.

description: Amiodarone increases digoxin levels by 70–100% through inhibition of P-glycoprotein and renal clearance reduction.
recommendation: Halve the digoxin dose when starting amiodarone. Monitor digoxin levels and ECG closely.

description: Clarithromycin inhibits P-glycoprotein, reducing digoxin clearance and raising serum levels to potentially toxic concentrations.
recommendation: Monitor digoxin levels and renal function during and after clarithromycin therapy.

description: Loop diuretics can cause hypokalaemia, which potentiates digoxin toxicity even at therapeutic digoxin levels.
recommendation: Monitor potassium and magnesium levels. Replace electrolytes as needed.

description: Combined serotonergic activity raises the risk of serotonin syndrome. Fluoxetine also inhibits CYP2D6, impairing tramadol activation.
recommendation: Avoid combination. Use an alternative analgesic or an SSRI with lower serotonin risk.

description: Additive serotonergic stimulation increases serotonin syndrome risk (agitation, hyperthermia, tachycardia, clonus).
recommendation: Avoid combination. If necessary, use the lowest effective doses and monitor closely.

description: Combination causes potentially life-threatening serotonin syndrome: hyperthermia, rigidity, rhabdomyolysis, seizures, death.
recommendation: Contraindicated. Allow at least 14 days washout after stopping an MAOI before starting an SSRI, and vice versa.

description: Ciprofloxacin dramatically increases tizanidine plasma levels (up to 10×), causing profound hypotension and sedation.
recommendation: Contraindicated. Use an alternative antibiotic if the patient is on tizanidine.

description: Ciprofloxacin inhibits CYP1A2, significantly raising theophylline levels and risk of seizures and arrhythmias.
recommendation: Reduce theophylline dose and monitor serum levels frequently.

description: Both drugs prolong the QT interval; combination substantially increases the risk of torsades de pointes.
recommendation: Avoid combination. Choose an alternative antibiotic without QT-prolonging effects.

description: Calcium supplements impair levothyroxine absorption from the GI tract, reducing thyroid hormone bioavailability.
recommendation: Separate administration by at least 4 hours.

description: Iron salts chelate levothyroxine in the gut, reducing its absorption by up to 50%.
recommendation: Take levothyroxine on an empty stomach, at least 4 hours before or after iron supplementation.

description: NSAIDs reduce renal lithium clearance, raising serum lithium to potentially toxic levels.
recommendation: Avoid NSAIDs in patients on lithium. Use acetaminophen. Monitor lithium levels if NSAID use is unavoidable.

description: ACE inhibitors reduce renal sodium and lithium excretion, potentially causing lithium toxicity.
recommendation: Monitor lithium levels closely when initiating or adjusting ACE inhibitor dose.

description: Additive nitric oxide–mediated vasodilation causes severe, potentially fatal hypotension.
recommendation: Absolutely contraindicated. Do not co-administer under any circumstances.

description: Same mechanism as sildenafil + nitrate: catastrophic hypotension risk.
recommendation: Absolutely contraindicated.

description: NSAIDs reduce renal methotrexate clearance, raising levels and risk of severe toxicity (bone marrow suppression, nephrotoxicity).
recommendation: Avoid NSAIDs with methotrexate, especially at higher doses. Use acetaminophen for analgesia.

description: Both drugs inhibit dihydrofolate reductase; combination produces severe, additive folate deficiency and bone marrow suppression.
recommendation: Avoid combination. If unavoidable, monitor CBC closely and administer folinic acid rescue.

description: Fluconazole inhibits CYP3A4 and CYP2C19, dramatically raising tacrolimus levels and increasing nephrotoxicity risk.
recommendation: Reduce tacrolimus dose by 50%. Monitor levels and renal function closely.

description: Cyclosporine inhibits CYP3A4 and OATP1B1/1B3 transporters, greatly increasing simvastatin levels and myopathy/rhabdomyolysis risk.
recommendation: Avoid combination. Use pravastatin or rosuvastatin at low doses if a statin is required.

description: Allopurinol inhibits xanthine oxidase, blocking azathioprine metabolism and raising levels to potentially fatal toxicity.
recommendation: If combination is necessary, reduce azathioprine dose by 75%. Monitor CBC closely.

description: Fluconazole inhibits CYP2C9, markedly increasing phenytoin plasma levels and risk of toxicity (nystagmus, ataxia, confusion).
recommendation: Monitor phenytoin levels closely. Reduce phenytoin dose if toxicity develops.

description: Erythromycin inhibits CYP3A4, raising carbamazepine levels and risk of toxicity (diplopia, ataxia, nausea).
recommendation: Monitor carbamazepine levels closely; use an alternative antibiotic if possible.

description: Alcohol inhibits gluconeogenesis and masks hypoglycaemia symptoms, increasing risk of severe unrecognised hypoglycaemia.
recommendation: Advise patient to eat before drinking and monitor blood glucose frequently.

description: Fluconazole inhibits CYP2C9, increasing glipizide levels and risk of severe hypoglycaemia.
recommendation: Monitor blood glucose closely and reduce glipizide dose if needed.

description: Antacids containing aluminium, calcium, or magnesium chelate doxycycline, reducing its absorption by up to 80%.
recommendation: Separate doxycycline from antacids by at least 2–3 hours.

description: Polyvalent cations in antacids form insoluble chelates with ciprofloxacin, dramatically reducing bioavailability.
recommendation: Take ciprofloxacin 2 hours before or 6 hours after antacids or mineral supplements.

description: Amiodarone inhibits CYP3A4 and P-glycoprotein, increasing atorvastatin exposure and myopathy risk.
recommendation: Use the lowest effective atorvastatin dose. Monitor for muscle pain or weakness.

description: Gemfibrozil inhibits OATP1B1 and glucuronidation of rosuvastatin, raising AUC by up to 4× and myopathy risk.
recommendation: Avoid combination. If necessary, limit rosuvastatin to 10 mg/day.

description: Combination of corticosteroid and NSAID substantially increases the risk of GI ulceration and bleeding.
recommendation: Use a proton pump inhibitor for gastric protection. Prefer acetaminophen for analgesia.

description: Both drugs are ototoxic; combination increases the risk of irreversible hearing loss and vestibular damage.
recommendation: Avoid concurrent use. If necessary, use the lowest effective doses and monitor hearing.

description: Chronic alcohol use induces CYP2E1, converting more acetaminophen to a hepatotoxic metabolite (NAPQI), increasing liver damage risk.
recommendation: Limit acetaminophen to ≤2 g/day in regular alcohol drinkers. Avoid entirely in heavy drinkers.
